item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose automatic priority for society over individual rights from the start through the final turn. Agent A slightly intensifies in turn 3 by stressing that public-interest language can silence weak or dissenting people, but returns to the same balanced rights-boundary position by turn 5. Agent B remains consistently firm, acknowledging gotong royong, public health, safety, and order only as tightly limited exceptions.
